full_prompt: Design a full riverine passenger ferry hull for an inland-waterway route roughly 200 km long. The hull must carry 150 passengers plus 6 crew plus 6,000 kg of cargo and baggage across a calm-to-moderate inland waterway with a minimum navigable depth of 2.0 m in the dry season. The hull is a welded marine-grade steel monohull of LOA 30.0 m, beam 8.0 m, depth molded 2.5 m, and design draft 1.4 m fully loaded. Hull structural material is ASTM A131 Grade A shipbuilding steel (yield 235 MPa, ultimate 400 MPa, E 200 GPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). Pass/fail criteria for the ferry as a whole: (R1) intact stability per IMO Resolution A.749 (18) - GMt must be at least 0.35 m and the righting arm GZ curve must show positive righting up to at least 40 degrees heel with area under GZ curve 0-30 degrees at least 0.055 m-rad, (R2) damage stability after one-compartment flooding per SOLAS 2020 Chapter II-1 must retain residual GMt at least 0.05 m and margin line at least 76 mm above the damaged waterline, (R3) minimum passenger deck freeboard in the fully loaded condition must be at least 760 mm per inland-waterway rule, (R4) hull longitudinal bending stress under still-water hogging condition must not exceed 157 MPa which is yield divided by 1.5, and (R5) average passenger deck area per passenger must be at least 0.45 m^2. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly of the ferry hull including watertight bulkheads, plus a stability booklet with GMt, GZ curve to 60 degrees, damage stability result, freeboard, longitudinal bending stress, and passenger-area allocation.
short_prompt:
Design the hull and watertight subdivision of a riverine passenger ferry: a welded shipbuilding-steel monohull, LOA 30.0 m x beam 8.0 m x molded depth 2.5 m, design draft 1.4 m, carrying 150 passengers + 6 crew + 6 t cargo across an inland waterway with 2 m minimum navigable depth.

Stability follows IMO Resolution A.749(18) intact and SOLAS 2020 Chapter II-1 damage stability; hull girder follows IACS UR S7 still-water bending.

Load cases.
- LC1 full-load intact: fully loaded 150 PAX + crew + cargo, still water.
- LC2 one-compartment damage: any single compartment flooded.
- LC3 still-water hog: hogging moment per IACS UR S7.

Also check inland-waterway passenger-deck freeboard and passenger-area allocation per PAX.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Intact and damage stability are closed-form hydrostatic + righting-arm calculations, not FEA.

prompt.title: 30 m inland passenger ferry, 150 PAX
prompt.brief: Design a 30.0 m x 8.0 m x 2.5 m steel monohull ferry for 150 PAX + 6 crew + 6 t cargo on an inland river, meeting IMO A.749 intact + SOLAS damage stability, 760 mm freeboard, hull girder stress, and passenger-area allocation.

material.name: ASTM A131 Grade A shipbuilding steel

load_cases.name: still_water_hog
load_cases.application: hogging moment per IACS UR S7

boundary_conditions.hull: freely floating in still water, weight + buoyancy balanced

objective.primary: pass intact + damage stability + freeboard + girder stress

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for plating, beam for framing

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: No seakeeping CFD required on inland river at low speed.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Not in pass/fail for this brief.

eval_coverage: IMO Resolution A.749 intact stability + SOLAS damage stability
passenger safety-driven freeboard and deck allocation
riverine ferry design with single-compartment damage
